description: Evaluates policy management maturity—reference subject/object access framework, rule lifecycle, software-defined and context-based domain policies (network, authentication, authorization, application, data), teams, KPIs/KRIs, SOC monitoring of policy changes, and AI-era policy governance.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A policy management service exists and is reviewed at least quarterly for efficacy.

capabilities.name: Reference policy framework (subject/object access matrix)
capabilities.description: A reference policy framework provides enterprise-wide visibility of who can access what, guides granular policies, and is reviewed at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Policy management KPIs and KRIs—including policy drift rate, rule exception count, and deployment success rate—are documented, effective enterprise-wide, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Rule deployment success and rollback metrics

capabilities.name: Rule management system
capabilities.description: Centralized rule management supports automation, policy-as-code, integration with decision-makers, and quarterly configuration review.

capabilities.name: Rule lifecycle management
capabilities.description: Rule creation, modification, and deletion are documented, enterprise-wide, automated where possible, and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Documented rule creation and modification process
Documented rule deletion and deprovisioning process

capabilities.name: Policy management teams
capabilities.description: Policy management teams are centralized or clearly defined with documented hierarchy and separation of duties.
evaluation_focus: Formalized team composition and separation of duties

capabilities.name: Software-defined policy
capabilities.description: Software-defined policy is maintained, deployed programmatically to enforcement points, automated, and validated at least quarterly.

capabilities.name: Context-based domain policies
capabilities.description: Context-based policies for network, authentication, authorization (including guest), application, and data access are deployed enterprise-wide and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Context-based network access control vs IP-only policies
Context-based authorization and guest policies enterprise-wide
Classification and context-based data access policies enterprise-wide
Quarterly review of domain context-based policies

capabilities.name: Policy change monitoring
capabilities.description: Rule and policy changes are formally logged and securely transferred to the SOC, including dynamic changes and anomalous bulk policy modifications.

capabilities.name: AI-era policy governance
capabilities.description: Policies govern approved versus shadow AI tool usage, data handling by AI systems, and delegated agent actions under EU AI Act and internal risk appetite.
evaluation_focus: Approved versus shadow AI tool policy boundaries
Data handling and retention policies for AI systems
Delegated agent action policies aligned to EU AI Act risk tiers

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
